L1.area: JSON parsing and object mapping layer

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where JSON data containing class metadata can cause arbitrary classes to be instantiated during the parsing process. When the parser is configured to use class metadata for polymorphic deserialization, there is no restriction on which classes can be loaded, allowing an attacker to supply crafted JSON that triggers instantiation of dangerous classes, potentially leading to remote code execution.

L3.area: JSON parser's map-to-bean conversion and class instantiation logic
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where JSON data containing class metadata names can cause arbitrary classes to be instantiated during the bean conversion process. When the class metadata name feature is enabled for polymorphic type handling, there is no whitelist to restrict which classes can be loaded from untrusted JSON input, allowing an attacker to supply crafted JSON that triggers instantiation of dangerous classes, potentially leading to remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: Jodd's JSON parser allows arbitrary class instantiation from untrusted JSON input when setClassMetadataName is configured, with no whitelist or restriction on loadable classes, enabling remote code execution via crafted class metadata.
